title: Autumn Durald Arkapaw Makes History at 2026 Oscars - Hollywood Reporter
text: Cinematographer Autumn Durald Arkapaw became the first woman of any race to win the Academy Award for Best Cinematography at the 2026 Oscars ceremony. She earned the recognition for her work on the film Sinners, directed by Ryan Coogler. Arkapaw, who is of Filipino and African American Creole descent, defeated fellow nominees Dan Laustsen (Frankenstein), Darius Khondji (Marty Supreme), Michael Bauman (One Battle After Another), and Adolpho Veloso (Train Dreams). During her remarks, Arkapaw thanked cinematographer Rachel Morrison, who had previously been nominated but never won. She acknowledged that I don't get here without you guys while inviting all women in the Dolby Theatre to stand up. Backstage, Arkapaw reflected on the achievement: a lot of little girls that look like me will sleep really well tonight, and I know that. She praised director Coogler for creating opportunities for women on set, noting he gives us those opportunities to shine and be ourselves in creative environments where they lead with trust and power. Sinners experienced significant Oscar success, with wins including Best Actor (Michael B. Jordan), Original Screenplay, and Original Score.
